objects.name: Analytic 1794
objects.description: A defender observes an application generating application-layer communications that blend with normal traffic (HTTP(S), WebSocket, DNS, mail protocols) but show deviations from enterprise baselines for that bundle ID—such as persistent background network sessions, regular low-volume polling intervals, anomalous SNI/Host destinations, uncommon DNS patterns, or uniform request/response sizing—suggesting command and control over legitimate-looking protocols without relying on tool signatures.

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: NSM:Flow
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Application-layer protocol traffic exhibiting beacon-like periodicity, anomalous session structure, or protocol misuse patterns

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: NSM:Flow
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Application-layer indicators observable via enterprise network controls (HTTP method, URI path pattern class, TLS SNI, JA3/ALPN when available, DNS qname/type) showing anomalous or low-and-slow command polling behavior

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Framework-based networking usage spikes or uncommon networking stacks observed by agent telemetry (e.g., repeated URLSession/OkHttp-like patterns) without corresponding foreground/user interaction

x_mitre_mutable_elements.description: Defines acceptable deviation in protocol communication timing
Baseline deviation tolerance for long-lived sessions
Expected mapping of application functionality to protocol usage
